home.name: Catharina van der Boor
home.lede: Senior Lecturer in Global Mental Health at LSHTM and Co-Director of the Centre for Health in Humanitarian Crises. I design and evaluate mental health interventions for populations affected by conflict and displacement.
anchors: Fieldwork in Uganda, Ukraine, Colombia, Sweden
Consultant to WHO, UNICEF (via IMC), Mid Sweden University

work.title: MHPSS trial — Uganda and Ukraine
work.body: A multi-country trial of a mental health and psychosocial support intervention for populations affected by conflict and displacement, conducted across sites in Uganda and Ukraine.

work.body: Cultural adaptation and psychometric validation of the Kankuamo Wellbeing Scale, developed with the Kankuamo Indigenous community in Colombia.

work.body: Mental health and psychosocial support needs assessment for UNICEF in Ukraine, informing programme design in a conflict-affected setting.

home.closing: Available for consultancy on MHPSS programme design, cultural adaptation of instruments, and implementation evaluation.

about.title: About
about.headshotAlt: Headshot of Catharina van der Boor
bio: Catharina van der Boor is Senior Lecturer in Global Mental Health and Co-Director of the Centre for Health in Humanitarian Crises at the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. Her work sits at the intersection of forced displacement, mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), and implementation science.

Her research focuses on the development and evaluation of culturally adapted diagnostic instruments and of scalable psychological interventions for displaced and underserved populations. She has authored peer-reviewed publications in leading journals and has secured competitive research funding for work spanning Europe, Africa, Latin America, and South Asia.

Alongside her academic appointment, she advises UN agencies, ministries of health, and international NGOs on programme design, evaluation, and capacity-building, with a methodological emphasis on mixed methods, psychometric validation, and implementation science.

timeline.role: Director of the Psychology Programme, Universitat Carlemany
Co-Director, Centre for Health in Humanitarian Crises, LSHTM
Senior Lecturer in Global Mental Health, LSHTM
Research Fellow in Global Mental Health, LSHTM
Postdoctoral Researcher in Global Mental Health, University of Liverpool
Research Data Coordinator, Médecins du Monde (United Kingdom)
MHPSS Programme Developer, Fundación Vicente Ferrer (India)

education.degree: PhD in Global Mental Health, University of Liverpool
education.detail: Thesis: "The Mental Health and Wellbeing of Asylum Seeking and Refugee Populations in High-Income Countries: A Capabilities Approach."

education.degree: MSc in Global Mental Health, University of Glasgow
BSc in Psychology and Neuroscience, Utrecht University

leadership: Associate Editor of Conflict and Health (2025–present); expert reviewer for The Lancet Psychiatry, Global Mental Health, Trials, and Social Psychiatry and Psychiatric Epidemiology.
Co-founder and board member of the Mental Health and Psychosocial Research Network; mentor to seven junior MHPSS professionals working in conflict-affected settings.
Member of the Examinations Board (LSHTM); external PhD examiner at King's College London, University of York, and Trinity College Dublin (2024–2026).
Faculty representative on the Gender Equity and Diversity Committee (LSHTM); technical advisor to the Government of Andorra (2023–2025).
Organiser of high-impact international seminars, including the webinar on public health institutions in Gaza (580 attendees; 78,900 views).

research.title: Research
research.metaDescription: Research on forced displacement, MHPSS, and implementation science. Current projects in Uganda, Ukraine, and Colombia.
research.intro: Catharina's research sits at the intersection of forced displacement, mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), and implementation science. She develops and evaluates culturally adapted diagnostic instruments and scalable psychological interventions for populations affected by conflict, displacement, and structural adversity, with a particular interest in how interventions perform when delivered at scale in low-resource and humanitarian settings.

projects.title: MHPSS trial — Uganda and Ukraine
projects.body: A multi-country trial evaluating a mental health and psychosocial support intervention for populations affected by conflict and displacement, with sites in Uganda and Ukraine.

projects.title: Kankuamo Wellbeing Scale — Colombia (£300k, UKRI / Minciencias)
projects.body: Cultural adaptation and psychometric validation of a wellbeing instrument co-developed with the Kankuamo Indigenous community, funded by UKRI in partnership with Minciencias.

projects.title: SPIDR consortium — Horizon Europe (under evaluation)
projects.body: Multi-partner European research consortium on mental health and psychosocial support for displaced populations. Proposal currently under evaluation by Horizon Europe.

research.methodsBody: Her work combines mixed methods, psychometric validation, systematic reviews, participatory action research, multivariable regression, and exploratory and principal component analysis (EFA / PCA).

consultancy.title: Consultancy
consultancy.metaDescription: Consultancy on MHPSS programme design, evaluation, cultural adaptation of instruments, and capacity building for UN agencies, INGOs, and ministries of health.
consultancy.intro: Catharina advises UN agencies, international NGOs, ministries of health, and academic partners on the design and evaluation of mental health and psychosocial support programmes. She supports the cultural adaptation and psychometric validation of measurement instruments, conducts needs assessments and implementation evaluations in conflict- and displacement-affected settings, and delivers training and capacity-building for research and programme teams. Engagements draw on the same methodological standards as her academic work — mixed methods, psychometric rigour, and an implementation science lens.

engagements.title: Short-term advisory
engagements.body: Targeted technical input on MHPSS programme design, study design, and methodological choices for time-bounded questions.

engagements.title: Evaluation and needs assessment
engagements.body: Mixed-methods evaluations and needs assessments in humanitarian and displacement-affected settings, from protocol to final report.

engagements.title: Training and capacity building
engagements.body: Training of research and programme teams in psychometric validation, qualitative methods, and implementation evaluation.

publications.title: Publications
publications.lead: 19 peer-reviewed publications across Trials, Transcultural Psychiatry, Conflict and Health, Global Mental Health, BMC Public Health, and others, plus a technical review for Cambridge University Press.
